mainEntity.name: How much does product photography cost in Warwickshire?

acceptedAnswer.text: Product photography in Warwickshire typically costs between £15 and £30 per product for white background packshots, with bulk discounts available for larger ranges. Lifestyle photography sessions run from around £350 for a half-day to £1,400 or more for a full-day commercial shoot. Ghost mannequin photography for clothing sits between £20 and £45 per garment. Always request a written quote that specifies exactly what's included, such as the number of angles, retouching, and file formats delivered.

mainEntity.name: What's the difference between packshot and lifestyle product photography?

acceptedAnswer.text: Packshot product photography shows your product on a clean white or neutral background, clearly and accurately. It's what Amazon requires for main listing images. Lifestyle photography shows the product in a real-world setting or in use, which is better for social media, advertising, and website hero images. Most Warwickshire ecommerce businesses benefit from having both.

mainEntity.name: How many product images do I need for my ecommerce listing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Leading brands use an average of 8 images per product. At minimum you need one clean front-facing packshot, one back or side view, and one detail shot. Amazon allows up to nine images per listing, and using more high-quality images consistently increases click-through and conversion rates.

mainEntity.name: Do I need product photography if I sell on Amazon?

acceptedAnswer.text: Amazon requires a pure white background for main product images, with the product filling at least 85% of the frame. Beyond compliance, listings with professional product photography receive higher click-through rates and convert far more effectively. Poor images on Amazon also lower your listing's organic search ranking over time.

mainEntity.name: How long does a product photography shoot take?

acceptedAnswer.text: A professional photographer working through a well-prepared shot list can typically shoot 20 to 30 products per day in a studio setting. Post-production editing typically takes as long as the shoot itself. From shoot day to final file delivery, most Warwickshire product photographers work to a 5 to 10 working day turnaround.

mainEntity.name: Is product photography worth it for a small business?

acceptedAnswer.text: Shopify research found products with professional images see a 33% higher conversion rate on average. If your product page converts at 1.5% and professional photography lifts that to 2% across 5,000 monthly visitors, that's 25 extra sales per month. For most small businesses, professional product photography is one of the highest-ROI marketing investments available.

mainEntity.name: Can I prepare my own products for a product photography shoot?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Clean all surfaces with a microfibre cloth to remove dust and fingerprints. Iron or steam fabric products. Remove price stickers and unwanted labels. Prepare a clear shot list before the session listing each product, the angles required, and specific details to capture. Good preparation reduces shoot time and keeps costs down.

mainEntity.name: Can Alan Ranger Photography train my staff to do product photography in-house?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Alan Ranger Photography offers corporate photography training for business teams that want to build an in-house product photography capability. Training covers lighting basics, camera settings, background setup, and basic post-processing — ideal for businesses with a frequently changing product range.
